Question: In the circuits shown in figure, the parameters of both circuits are identical and both are in resonance. If the frequency of the power supply increases, then:
Choices 
A. Both light bulbs in the two figures will become brighter  
B. Both light bulbs in the two figures will become dimmer  
C. The light bulb in the left figure will become brighter, and the light bulb in the right figure will become dimmer  
D. The light bulb in the left figure will become dimmer, and the light bulb in the right figure will become brighter

Question: As shown in the image, a rectangular region contains a uniform and steady magnetic field. A semicircular closed wire loop rotates counterclockwise with constant angular velocity around axis \( O \) in the plane of the paper. The point \( O \) is the center of the semicircle and is located exactly at the edge of the magnetic field. Time measurement starts when the semicircular loop is completely outside the magnetic field. Among the graphs (A)-(D), which \( -t \) function graph corresponds to the induced electromotive force generated in the semicircular wire loop?

Choices:  
A. graph A  
B. graph B  
C. graph C  
D. graph D

Question: The figure shows the single line diagram of a single machine infinite bus system.

The inertia constant of the synchronous generator \( H = 5 \ \text{MW-s/MVA} \). Frequency is 50 Hz. Mechanical power is 1 pu. The system is operating at the stable equilibrium point with rotor angle \( \delta \) equal to \( 30^\circ \). A three-phase short circuit fault occurs at a certain location on one of the circuits of the double circuit transmission line. During fault, electrical power in pu is \( P_{\text{max}} \sin \delta \). If the values of \( \delta \) and \( \frac{d\delta}{dt} \) at the instant of fault clearing are \( 45^\circ \) and 3.762 radian/s respectively, then \( P_{\text{max}} \) (in pu) is ______.

Choices:
A. \( 0.11 \)  
B. \( 0.23 \)  
C. \( 0.48 \)  
D. \( 0.64 \)
